rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM H. QUAYLE TRUST DATED NOV. 11, 1980
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include: 1) Undistributed tangible personal items; 2) Reimbursement to Marky Quayle and sale of real property; and 3) Trustee's fees. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ANTHONY SANCHEZ SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #9, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. please review the 6th amended general order for information regarding remote appearances at https://www.sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate ISSUES (to be addressed at hearing): 1) Petitioner requests to modify the trust pay back provisions to Medi-Cal. 2) Amendment requests an increase in monthly allowance to parent from $500.00 to $700.00. Examiner requested 1 receipt for $160.00 for entertainment. No receipt was provided and claims no receipts are retained. 3) Petitioner is requesting authority to retain the mutual funds on hand at end of the account period. 4) Trustee requests $8,282.80 in fees. 5) Attorney is requesting $8,050.00 in fees and $551.00 in costs.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GRADY LOVETT 2011 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Establish The Equitable Adoption Of Princess Green By Grady Lovett
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The sole issue for the hearing is whether the relief sought by this petition should be adjudicated in the probate department. No argument on the merits will be heard. If the Court determines that this petition should be heard, a separate hearing will be set for the Court's determination of the merits. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LINDA CARDEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/31/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) Courtesy copies and proposed orders have not been received by the assigned examiner (counsel notified 08/10/2020). 2) A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: A) Item 8 of the petition needs to clearly identify all trustees of the devisee trust. If the petitioner is the sole trustee, beneficiaries of the trust within the meaning of Probate Code Sec 1208(b) must be clearly identified. See also LRSF 14.26(D)(2). B) The names and dates of death of the decedent's spouse and father must be specified. LRSF 14.26(E)(3). C) Did the decedent's mother request that notice be given to the P.O. Box address listed in item 8? See Sec 1220(a)(3). D) Item 8 needs to list all issue of the predeceased spouse's parents. E) Has the decedent left any memorandum or other similar writing set forth in Section 1.01 of the will? If so, a copy must be filed.
